DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS DAWN MASON AND JAMES MASON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE IS CONTINUED TO 1/24/19 @ 9 AM IN DEPT 301. AFTER REVIEWING THE EXTENSIVE BRIEFING BY BOTH SIDES, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PARTIES NEED TO CONDUCT A MUCH MORE THOROUGH MEET AND CONFER BY TELEPHONE (NOT LETTER) TO ATTEMPT TO RESOLVE THE ISSUES RAISED IN THE MOTION. ALSO, THE PARTIES ARE STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO DISCUSS A POSSIBLE STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER IF IT IS AGREED ONE WILL BE NECESSARY IN THIS CASE. THE TELEPHONIC MEET AND CONFER SHALL OCCUR BY 1/11/19. NO LATER THAN 1/17/19, BOTH PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO SEND AN EMAIL TO JPT MCDONALD AT THE EMAIL ADDRESS IN THIS TENTATIVE ADVISING OF THE RESULTS OF THE MEET AND CONFER (WITH SPECIFIC REFERENCES TO THE RFPDS AT ISSUE) AND THE MATTERS REMAINING TO BE ADDRESSED BY THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON 1/24/19 TO DAVIDMCDONALD@PRODIGY.NET. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Continued To Mar-30-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Leave To File Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caitlin Scott, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAWN MASON, JAMES MASON's Motion Requesting Leave To File Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiffs Dawn Mason and James Mason's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion conforms to California's strong policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings and Defendants have not shown that they would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. (See Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) Defendant's objections to the amendments can be addressed through pleading motions. Defendant has not shown that a trial continuance is warranted at this stage of the proceedings.

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Continued To Jan-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs DAWN MASON and JAMES MASON's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is Continued to 1/24/19 @ 9 am in Dept 301. After reviewing the extensive briefing by both sides, the Court finds that the parties need to conduct a much more thorough meet and confer by telephone (not letter) to attempt to resolve the issues raised in the motion. Also, the parties are strongly encouraged to discuss a possible stipulated protective order if it is agreed one will be necessary in this case. The telephonic meet and confer shall occur by 1/11/19. No later than 1/17/19, both parties are directed to send an email to JPT McDonald at the email address in this tentative advising of the results of the meet and confer (with specific references to the RFPDS at issue) and the matters remaining to be addressed by the Court at the hearing on 1/24/19 to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
